# Comment-hygiene purge playbook
<!-- read-receipt-token: e18c5973 -->

## When this fires (no user invocation needed)

Auto-fires as post-code-change step 2.4, AFTER edits land and BEFORE the §2.6 comment audit and the multi-model panel (step 3). It is a genuinely new procedure step (the em-dash scan is the closest analog, but that is a CI / `emdash-scan` ledger check with no procedure step). Scope is the **whole of every touched file**, not just the diff lines: a touched file's pre-existing comments are in scope alongside the ones the diff added.

The sweep's own action is **DELETE-only**: enumerate every comment in each touched file, classify it, and DELETE the ones that fail the rubric. Removal needs no per-comment `ask_user` because `comment-protocol.md`'s `deletion` is already an exempt category (removing a comment never enters the add-gate) - that exempt rule, NOT "mechanicalness," is why the sweep skips step 3. The CLASSIFY decision is semantic judgment; the ACTION is deletion.

### Scope - which files, which comments

- **Files:** the files the change touched in the working tree, classified like the surrounding phase: `git diff --name-only` (unstaged) + `git diff --cached --name-only` (staged) + (clean feature branch) `git diff --name-only <merge-base>..HEAD`, plus relevant untracked new files. Do NOT use a bare `<base>..HEAD` (it misses uncommitted edits).
- **Comments:** every comment syntax enumerated in `comment-protocol.md` §Scope (the canonical scanner definition - line, block, doc-comment, and docstring forms across all supported languages), new AND pre-existing.
- **Excluded files (mirror `comment-protocol.md` exempt categories):** `generated` (`<auto-generated>` header / known generated-output paths) and `vendored` / third-party (`vendor/`, `third_party/`). On a semi-generated partial, the hand-maintained side IS in scope.

## Never purge - functional / tool-consumed comments (these are not explanatory)

A comment whose ABSENCE breaks a compiler, linter, type-checker, coverage tool, or runtime framework - or that carries semantic DATA a tool requires - is functional, not explanatory; leave it. **When unsure whether a comment is consumed, KEEP it.** (Narrative doc prose is NOT auto-exempt merely because a doc generator would render it: a restatement `<summary>` still follows the Delete/Keep rubric below, and is only HELD - not "never purged" - when the build-safety pre-check finds a docs-required-at-error signal.) Non-exhaustive functional set:

- **Shebangs** (`#!/usr/bin/env ...`).
- **Build / codegen directives**: Go `//go:build`, `// +build`, `//go:embed`, `//go:generate`; C# `#pragma`, `#region`/`#endregion`.
- **Suppression / lint directives**: `// eslint-disable*`, `// @ts-ignore` / `@ts-expect-error`, `// prettier-ignore`, `/* istanbul ignore next */`, `# noqa`, `# type: ignore`, `# pylint: disable`, `# pragma: no cover`, `//nolint`, `# rubocop:disable`, `// NOSONAR`, C# `[SuppressMessage]` / analyzer-suppression comments.
- **Runtime/compile-consumed docstrings**: Python docstrings surfaced as CLI/API help (argparse / click / Typer / FastAPI), Elixir `@doc` / `@moduledoc`, and any docstring read as data at runtime or compile time.
- **Copyright / license headers** and the canonical `THROWAWAY: <prototype-name>` header on files under `prototypes/<name>/` (per `comment-protocol.md` §Scope-exempt).

## Relationship to `comment-protocol.md` (no overlap, no contradiction)

- `comment-protocol.md` governs **ADDING** a comment: the clarity -> rename -> `ask_user` gate on every NEW or substantively-rewritten comment line, at edit time.
- THIS purge governs **DELETING** comments already present in a touched file. It runs once, at step 2.4.

"Compress a novel to one terse line" WITHOUT an in-place rewrite:

- A multi-line comment that fails the rubric is **DELETED** (the purge's only action). If its one load-bearing why genuinely cannot be carried by a rename / signature (AGENTS.md §3.1 *Allowed*), the agent then **adds a fresh one-line comment via `comment-protocol.md` step 3** (the add-gate already runs in this phase). So "compress" = purge-delete + add-gate-add; the purge never substantively rewrites in place, and the add-gate stays the single authority for any surviving text.
- **Degraded-mode / no-response HOLD (do not silently lose an invariant):** deleting a restatement / narration comment is unconditional (nothing is lost). Deleting a multi-line comment that carries a load-bearing why is CONDITIONAL on the one-line replacement being addable. If the add-gate cannot run - degraded mode, no-response, or step-3 rejection (`comment-protocol.md` §Degraded mode / Step 3) - **HOLD the original comment** (count it as held) rather than delete-then-fail-to-re-add. This prevents the headless/CI path from silently deleting a security / correctness / ordering invariant.

## The rubric

**Default: NO comment.** Delete any comment that merely restates what well-named code already says. A comment survives ONLY when the code genuinely cannot express the intent - and then it is held to **one terse line of semantic content, never a paragraph / "novel"** (syntax-required tag structure or a directive's own multi-line form is exempt from the one-line shape; the BAN is on multi-line narrative prose).

### Delete
- XML `<summary>` / `<param>` / `<returns>` / `<remarks>` (and JSDoc / non-consumed docstring equivalents) that just narrate a well-named type / method / parameter.
- Section-header, step (`// 1) ...`), and narration comments.
- Restatement comments (`// increment the counter`). If deleting one exposes a poorly-named symbol, rely on the normal §3.3 rename-refactor - do NOT keep the restatement.
- Multi-line rationale the code + names already make obvious.

### Keep - one terse line of semantic content
Non-obvious **why** only: native-interop quirks, struct-pack / marshalling requirements, security / correctness invariants, ordering guarantees (e.g. "first channel wins"), workarounds, deliberate no-ops, lifetime / ownership rationale on a no-op dispose, bounds / cap rationale on hostile input. These map to the three AGENTS.md §3.1 *Allowed* cases. A surviving why that REPLACES a deleted novel is added through `comment-protocol.md` step 3.

## Calibration example (illustrative pseudonyms - not a real type)

**Before** (the load-bearing "why" lives ONLY inside an XML novel):

```csharp
/// <summary>
///     A lightweight cursor at one position inside an owning <see cref="Owner" />. All reads delegate back
///     to the owner against this cursor's offset. <see cref="Dispose" /> is intentionally a no-op: the
///     <see cref="Owner" /> owns the memory mapping, so disposing a cursor (as the readers do per
///     <c>using</c>) must NOT tear down the shared view.
/// </summary>
internal sealed class ResourceCursor(Owner owner, uint offset) : IReadCursor
{
    public void Dispose() { }
}
```

**After** (purge DELETES the XML novel; the one non-obvious why is re-added as a terse `//` via the add-gate):

```csharp
internal sealed class ResourceCursor(Owner owner, uint offset) : IReadCursor
{
    // No-op: the owner owns the mapping; a cursor owns nothing.
    public void Dispose() { }
}
```

## Build-safety pre-check (before deleting exported doc comments)

Deleting exported doc comments can break a build that REQUIRES docs.

- **.NET:** before deleting any public `///` block, scan for `GenerateDocumentationFile=true` combined with CS1591 promoted to an error through ANY route - `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `WarningsAsErrors` containing `CS1591`, `.editorconfig` `dotnet_diagnostic.CS1591.severity = error`, or a StyleCop SA16xx doc rule at error - in the project, `Directory.Build.props`/`.targets`, or `.editorconfig`.
- **Other languages:** check the equivalent docs-required signal (Rust `#![deny(missing_docs)]` on a `pub` item, Java javadoc doclint with `-Werror`, ESLint `require-jsdoc` at error, pydocstyle/ruff `D` at error).
- When such a signal is present, or you are unsure, **HOLD** those exported doc comments (count them as held). The post-code-change build + tests (step 7) are the authoritative backstop; this pre-check just avoids a wasted build cycle.

## Procedure

1. Enumerate touched files per *Scope* (exclude generated / vendored).
2. For each, scan EVERY comment (new + pre-existing) - not just diff lines.
3. Classify each: Delete / Keep-one-line / Never-purge (functional).
4. Apply the build-safety pre-check; HOLD exported docs where a docs-required signal is present.
5. DELETE the failing comments. For a deleted novel whose why is load-bearing, add a one-line replacement via `comment-protocol.md` step 3; if the add-gate cannot run, HOLD the original instead.
6. Emit the sweep status on the ledger (below).

## Recording (single authority - no double-bookkeeping)

Purge DELETIONS are attested ONLY as a COUNT on the new `comment-hygiene-purge` ledger row. They are NOT additionally emitted as §2.6 `n/a - exempt: deletion` bullets (the `deletion` exempt category remains for non-purge, edit-time removals). Any one-line REPLACEMENT the agent adds is a new comment line and IS recorded by §2.6 through the add-gate. When the purge removed comments but added no new/rewritten lines, §2.6 reports `ran, 0 new-or-rewritten` (NOT N/A - comments WERE removed). This keeps one per-site authority (§2.6 for added/rewritten) and one sweep attestation (the purge row for removals); the two never record the same site.

POST-CODE-CHANGE LEDGER `comment-hygiene-purge` gate row (canonical schema + KV field in `review-workflow-gates-sweeps.md` §2B, placed adjacent to `emdash-scan`):

```
comment-hygiene-purge: <ran, N removed, H held | N/A: no comments in touched files | N/A: only generated/vendored files touched>
```

`H` is the build-safety / degraded-mode held count (0 when none). Replacement one-liners are not counted here - they live in §2.6.

KV v1 field (fixed arity - always emit `N/H`): `purge=<ran:N/H | na:no-comments-in-touched-files | na:generated-vendored-only>`.

## Reviewer enforcement (panel, step 3) + catalog rule

The post-code-change panel target is `diff`, but the purge governs pre-existing comments on UNCHANGED lines. So instruct the panel to **open EACH touched file in FULL** (comments on unchanged lines are in scope), not just the diff hunks, and flag: any surviving comment that restates the code, any multi-line XML / doc narrative summary on a self-evident member, any kept comment whose semantic content exceeds one line without a load-bearing why. A surviving restatement comment is a BLOCKING finding (the purge missed it). This is enforced by the `comment-hygiene-purge-survivor` catalog slug (review-pass-only).

**Held-doc carve-out (not a survivor finding):** a doc comment the purge deliberately HELD - because the build-safety pre-check found a docs-required-at-error signal on that member, or a degraded-mode / no-response HOLD applied to a multi-line load-bearing why - is OUT of survivor scope. The `comment-hygiene-purge-survivor` rule does NOT raise a SURVIVOR finding on a narrative doc summary on a member subject to a docs-required-at-error signal; a correctly-held doc is not a survivor miss. This carve-out narrows ONLY this slug's finding - `comment-necessity` and every other rule are unaffected (and `comment-necessity` already excludes pre-existing comments, so it never flags a held pre-existing doc either; a NEW public-API doc is still governed by `comment-protocol.md`'s add-gate + `comment-necessity`). A degraded-mode / no-response HOLD only ever applies to a multi-line load-bearing why (per §Relationship "Degraded-mode / no-response HOLD"); restatement / narration is deleted UNCONDITIONALLY even in degraded mode and remains a BLOCKING survivor if any escaped. (The `H` held count on the purge row is the aggregate signal; a member under a docs-required-at-error build is the per-site discriminator the reviewer applies.)

## Implementation checklist (wiring - for the diff review, not the running agent)

- NEW `.github/playbooks/comment-hygiene-purge.md` (this file).
- AGENTS.md §3.1: terse pointer bullet; workflow-router AUTO-FIRE row.
- post-code-change.md: step 2.4 (before §2.6 + panel); add `purge=` to the worked KV example; the §2.6 / hard-gates note that deletion-only purge yields §2.6 `ran, 0`.
- review-workflow-gates-sweeps.md §2B: `comment-hygiene-purge` ledger row adjacent to `emdash-scan`; `purge=` in the KV v1 grammar; a `comment-hygiene-purge` bullet in the §"Skip conditions" list with the two N/A reasons; reconcile `comment-audit-§3.1` deletion wording.
- comment-protocol.md: one-line note that purge removals are attested on the purge row (the `deletion` exempt category stays for non-purge edit-time removals).
- pattern-catalog.sources/00-catalog.md: NEW `comment-hygiene-purge-survivor` review-pass-only slug (tier MEDIUM) carrying the full-file survivor instruction + the held-doc carve-out; then regenerate (`generate-pattern-catalog.ps1`), verify (`verify-pattern-catalog.ps1`), sync (`sync-critical-rules.ps1`).
- scripts/tests/check-post-code-change.tests.ps1: add `purge=` to the KV-keyset drift-guard loop (~line 247).
- manifest.yaml: new playbook entry; `.github/pr-quality-gate/read-receipts.tsv`: add the playbook path (convention).
